---
title: "Epidemic Markov Models"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{EpidemicMarkovModels}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)

```



Computes the exact probability distribution of the number of susceptibles
infected during an outbreak, using the embedded discrete-time Markov chain
of the continuous-time stochastic SIR epidemic. At each step, either one
susceptible becomes infected or one infective recovers, with probabilities
proportional to their respective rates. Absorption probabilities are derived
from the canonical form of the transition matrix.


# Epidemic Markov Models


## Continuous time models


## Discrete time models



```{r setup}
library(outbreakdistr)





```
